title: Method, apparatus and computer program product for entering operational states based on an input type
abstract: A method is provided for providing various operational states based on user input type. In particular, an example method may include providing for operation in a first operational state (300), receiving a user input (310), and determining if the user input is of a first input type (315). A second operational state based on the user input may be determined in response to the user input being of the first input type (330). Methods may include providing for operation in the second operational state, different from the first operational state, in response to receiving the user input of the first input type (340), where the second operational state precludes input of a second input type and allows input of a first input type (350), where the first and second input types are different from one another.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A method comprising: providing for operation in a first operational state; receiving a user input; determining if the user input is of a first input type; determining a second operational state based on the user input in response to the user input being of the first input type; and providing for operation in the second operational state, different from the first operational state, in response to receiving the user input of the first input type, wherein the second operational state precludes input of a second input type and allows input of a first input type, wherein the second input type comprises an input type different from the first input type. 2. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: determining if the user input is of a second input type; determining a third operational state based on the user input in response to the user input being of the second input type; and providing for operation in the third operational state, different from the first operational state, in response to receiving the user input of the second input type, wherein the third operational state precludes input of the first input type and allows input of a second input type. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the first input type comprises an audio input type, and wherein the second input type comprises a touch input type. 4. The method of claim 1, further comprising changing the available input type in response to a detected environmental condition 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the first input type comprises a motion input type, and wherein the second operational state provides output in the form of a tactile or haptic output. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the second operational state precludes input of any input type other than the first input type. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the first operational state is a locked state, wherein a transition from the first operational state to the second operational state is performed only in response to a predetermined input sequence of the first input type. 8. The method of claim 2, wherein the first operational state is a locked state, wherein a transition from the first operational state to the third operational state is performed only in response to a predetermined input sequence of the second input type, and wherein each of the second operational state and the third operational states comprise unlocked operational states that enable user interaction. 9. The method of claim 1, further comprising: determining a context, wherein determining a second operational state based on the user input and the first input type comprises determining a second operational state based on the user input, the first input type, and the context. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the second operational state comprises: at least one input type that is permitted; at least one input type that is precluded; at least one information output type that is permitted; and at least one information output type that is precluded. 11. The method of of claim 9, wherein the at least one information output type that is permitted is a visual output type, and wherein the at least one information output type that is precluded is an audio output type. 12. The method of of claim 9, wherein the at least one information output type that is permitted is a haptic output type, and wherein the at least one information output type that is precluded is a visible output type. 13. A computer program product comprising at least one non-transitory computer- readable storage medium having computer-executable program code instructions stored therein, the computer-executable program code instructions comprising program code instructions to perform a method comprising: 

 providing for operation in a first operational state; receiving a user input; determining if the user input is of a first input type; determining a second operational state based on the user input in response to the user input being of the first input type; and providing for operation in the second operational state, different from the first operational state, in response to receiving the user input of the first input type, wherein the second operational state precludes input of a second input type and allows input of a first input type, wherein the second input type comprises an input type different from the first input type. 14. An apparatus comprising at least one processor and at least one memory including computer program code, the at least one memory and the computer program code configured to, with the at least one processor, cause the apparatus to: provide for operation in a first operational state; receive a user input; determine if the user input is of a first input type; determine a second operational state based on the user input in response to the user input being of the first input type; and provide for operation in the second operational state, different from the first operational state, in response to receiving the user input of the first input type, wherein the second operational state precludes input of a second input type and allows input of a first input type, wherein the second input type comprises an input type different from the first input type. 15. The apparatus of claim 14, where in the at least one memory and the computer program code are further configured to, with the at least one processor, cause the apparatus to: determine if the user input is of a second input type; determine a third operational state based on the user input in response to the user input being of the second input type; and provide for operation in the third operational state, different from the first operational state, in response to receiving the user input of the second input type, wherein the third operational state precludes input of the first input type and allows input of a second input type. 16. The apparatus of claim 14, wherein the first input type comprises an audio input type, and wherein the second input type comprises a touch input type. 17. The apparatus of claim 14, where in the at least one memory and the computer program code are further configured to, with the at least one processor, cause the apparatus to change the available input type in response to a detected environmental condition 18. The apparatus of claim 14, wherein the first input type comprises a motion input type, and wherein the second operational state provides output in the form of a tactile or haptic output. 19. The apparatus of claim 14, wherein the second operational state precludes input of any input type other than the first input type. 20. The apparauts of claim 14, wherein the first operational state is a locked state, wherein a transition from the first operational state to the second operational state is performed only in response to a predetermined input sequence of the first input type. 21. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the first operational state is a locked state, wherein a transition from the first operational state to the third operational state is performed only in response to a predetermined input sequence of the second input type, and wherein each of the second operational state and the third operational states comprise unlocked operational states that enable user interaction.
